body:
# Feature request

## Summary

[As documented](https://base-ui.com/react/components/menu#navigate-to-another-page), `Menu.Item`s can render a `<a href>` to navigate to a URL when clicked, instead of executing an action on the page. Such a menu item is highlightable in exactly the same ways that a regular menu item is, but it is not _activatable_  in the same ways: pressing the Space key doesn't navigate to the URL. It does, however, close the menu (assuming `closeOnClick` is not changed from its default value of `true`), which seems wrong. I feel like if nothing happens, it should not close the menu, but ideally, IMO it _should_ navigate to the URL, just like when pressing Enter.

[CodeSandbox](https://codesandbox.io/p/sandbox/naughty-mclean-p9zwrl)

## Examples in other libraries

Not sure.

## Motivation

Consistency in keyboard behavior for end users who may not understand the difference between menu items that are links and those that are not.

comments.body:
Here is a relevant thread https://github.com/w3c/aria/issues/1701

Making `<a>` activate with Space is problematic, because Space scrolls the viewport. Users might expect Space to scroll the viewport, even when an `<a>` is focused. However, we could say this behaviour should be different inside widgets, since users generally don't expect Space to scroll the viewport when focus is inside a widget.

The main blocker is that `:active` doesn't fire when pressing Space. So if we do this, we'll need to commit to handling custom pseudo classes via data attrs. We haven't made a decision there yet.

Thanks @colmtuite. From the thread you linked to:

> If you design a link like a button (which is not a good idea) you can already make the link activatable with SPACE. **This would not be a violation of any specification** - but also not required according to WCAG 2.1.1, since WCAG does not require a specific keyboard operation, but only any.

(Emphasis mine.) In the Menu's case, a link is styled like a menu item, so I would expect it to work like other menu items. But I hear your concerns and shall await your decision!
I find it strange that even with explicit `Menu.LinkItem`s (introduced in v1.2.0), space doesn't activate the link... seems inconsistent when every other item in a menu can be activated with space.

Given the competition: [react-aria](https://react-aria.adobe.com/Menu#links), [fluent UI](https://storybooks.fluentui.dev/react/?path=/docs/components-menu-menu--docs#menu-item-link-navigation) I would agree that activating a `MenuLinkItem` with <kbd>Space</kbd> makes sense.
We can keep the option of disabling this behavior with the `event.preventBaseUIHandler()`.
WDYT guys?
cc @mj12albert
Yep, it will be changed in https://github.com/mui/base-ui/pull/4053

This fix will be available in the next npm release of Base UI.

In the meantime, you can try it out on our Canary release channel:

```sh
npm i https://pkg.pr.new/@base-ui/react@4053
```

labels.name: has workaround
labels.description: There’s a bug, but users have a complete workaround, so no urgent fix or release is needed.

title: [menu] Should link menu items navigate to their `href` when pressing Space?
